codeReview.summary: Re-run review confirms the prior blocking issue (env vars not applied to spawned process) remains UNFIXED. The code has not been modified to pass env vars from McpServerConfig.env to the spawned MCP server process. McpClient::connect() does not call .envs() or .env() on the Command, and the API design makes it impossible to pass env through without changes.

issues.description: Environment variables from McpServerConfig.env are still NOT applied when spawning the server process. McpClient::connect() spawns with tokio::process::Command::new(&program).args(&args) but never calls .envs() or .env(). The flow: McpConfigManager::start_server() -> McpClient::new(server_config.to_command_string()) where to_command_string() discards env entirely -> client.connect() spawns without any env vars. This is the same issue identified in the prior review and has not been fixed.
Unused variable 'base_dir' - the code computes base_dir from config_path.parent() with comment '// suppress unused warning' but base_dir is never used. Code smell that should be cleaned up.
McpServerConfig::to_command_string() discards the env HashMap entirely - it only concatenates command and args. The env field is parsed and stored but never used in process spawning.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix feature mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix was created in features.json but has no worker session and remains pending. No implementation has occurred.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix with workerSessionIds: [] and status: pending

sharedStateObservations.observation: McpClient API only accepts server_url (command string) in new(), making it impossible to pass env vars through without API changes. A proper fix requires either: (1) extending McpClient::new() to accept env and call .envs() in connect(), or (2) McpConfigManager storing env and applying it via a separate mechanism before/after connect().
sharedStateObservations.evidence: McpClient::new(server_url: impl Into<String>) has no env parameter - mcp.rs:274

sharedStateObservations.observation: McpConfigManager holds the full server_config (including env) but the start_server() method at line ~265 only passes to_command_string() to McpClient::new(). The env field is completely discarded at this boundary.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mcp_config.rs:265 - client = McpClient::new(server_config.to_command_string()); env field never transferred

summary: Re-run review confirms the blocking issue persists. Environment variables from McpServerConfig.env are NOT applied when spawning the MCP server process. The McpClient::connect() method does not call .envs() or .env() on the Command, and the to_command_string() method discards env entirely. The fix feature (mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix) was created but never started - no code changes have been made to address this issue.
